texto: Two hundred and fifty years of nameless, faceless, forgotten individuals. Yes, they were America's founding fathers and mothers as much as the bewigged white men who laid the whips upon their backs. Why didn't Lina know their names? Why hadn't she studied their histories? Where was the monument? Where was the museum? What had they wished for and worked for and loved?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Tara Conklin que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Two hundred and fifty years of nameless, faceless, forgotten individuals. Yes, they were America'…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, historical-fiction, the-house-girl, com foco em trabalho e the-house-girl.
